name: Linux Kernel
description: The Linux kernel is the free, open-source Unix-like kernel at the heart of every Linux distribution.

name: Debian
description: Debian is a free, general-purpose operating system developed by a worldwide volunteer community. It is the foundation for hundreds of other distributions including Ubuntu.

name: Devuan
description: Devuan is a fork of Debian that ships without systemd, offering users a choice of init systems including SysVinit, OpenRC, and runit.

name: Knoppix
description: Knoppix is one of the first popular live Linux distributions, built on Debian and designed to boot from CD, DVD, or USB stick without installation.

name: Damn Small Linux
description: Damn Small Linux was a tiny, ~50 MB live-CD distribution based on Knoppix, designed to run on low-spec or older hardware.

name: siduction
description: siduction is a community distribution based directly on Debian's unstable branch, providing regular semi-rolling snapshots with multiple desktop options.

name: antiX
description: antiX is a lightweight, systemd-free Debian derivative for older or low-resource computers, using IceWM and other tiny window managers.

name: MX Linux
description: MX Linux is a cooperative venture between antiX and MEPIS communities, producing a fast, lightweight Debian-based distro with custom tools.

name: Raspberry Pi OS
description: Raspberry Pi OS is the official operating system for the Raspberry Pi single-board computers, derived from Debian and tailored for ARM hardware.

name: BunsenLabs
description: BunsenLabs Linux is a community continuation of CrunchBang, offering a lightweight Debian-based Openbox desktop experience.

name: CrunchBang++
description: CrunchBang++ was a community continuation of CrunchBang Linux built on Debian, featuring Openbox and a minimalist philosophy.

name: deepin
description: Deepin is a Debian-based distribution from China featuring a polished, homegrown DDE desktop environment. It is developed by Wuhan Deepin Technology.

name: Pardus
description: Pardus is a Debian-based distribution developed by the Scientific and Technological Research Council of Turkey (TÜBİTAK).

name: SparkyLinux
description: SparkyLinux is a lightweight Debian-based distribution offering both rolling and stable editions with multiple desktop environments.

name: SolydXK
description: SolydXK is a Debian-based distribution aimed at home and office use, available in SolydX (XFCE) and SolydK (KDE) editions.

name: Solus
description: Solus began as a Debian-based distribution before going independent; it now ships its own eopkg package manager and the Budgie desktop by default.

name: Trisquel
description: Trisquel is a fully free-software GNU/Linux distribution based on Ubuntu (and now Debian) endorsed by the FSF, containing only FSF-approved components.

name: Trisquel Mini
description: Trisquel Mini is a lightweight edition of Trisquel using LXDE, suited to older or low-resource hardware while remaining fully free software.

name: PureOS
description: PureOS is a privacy-focused, FSF-endorsed Debian-based distribution developed by Purism for use on its Librem laptops and phones.

name: Endless OS
description: Endless OS is a Debian-based, OSTree-immutable distribution designed to work offline out of the box, with bundled content for emerging markets.

name: Porteus Kiosk
description: Porteus Kiosk is a locked-down, web-only Linux distribution based on Debian, designed for use as a public kiosk or thin client.

name: Proxmox VE
description: Proxmox Virtual Environment is an open-source server virtualization platform based on Debian, combining KVM and LXC with a web management interface.

name: Clonezilla
description: Clonezilla is a Debian-based partition and disk imaging/cloning suite similar to Norton Ghost, used for bare-metal backup and deployment.

name: Q4OS
description: Q4OS is a Debian-based distribution offering a fast, lightweight KDE Plasma or Trinity desktop with a Windows-like look for easy migration.

name: Whonix
description: Whonix is a security- and privacy-focused Debian derivative that routes all traffic through Tor using a gateway/workstation isolation design.

name: Tails
description: Tails is a live, amnesic operating system based on Debian that routes all traffic through Tor and leaves no trace on the host machine when shut down.

name: Kali Linux
description: Kali Linux is a Debian-derived distribution maintained by Offensive Security, preloaded with hundreds of tools for penetration testing and security auditing.

name: Kali NetHunter
description: Kali NetHunter is a mobile penetration-testing platform built on Kali Linux that runs as a chroot on Android devices with a custom kernel and tool overlay.

name: Parrot OS
description: Parrot Security OS is a Debian-based distribution aimed at security testing, forensics, and development, with built-in anonymity and privacy tooling.

name: Parrot Security
description: Parrot Security is the security-focused edition of Parrot OS, a Debian-based distribution for penetration testing, forensics, and reverse engineering.

name: Parrot Home
description: Parrot Home is the everyday-use edition of Parrot OS, aimed at daily computing while keeping the privacy and security tooling of the parent distro.

name: AV Linux
description: AV Linux is an Ubuntu-based distribution aimed at audio and video production, with a curated set of creative tools and a low-latency kernel.

name: Neptune
description: Neptune is a Debian-based distribution focused on a polished KDE Plasma desktop experience, decoupled from Ubuntu's release cycle.

name: SolusOS
description: SolusOS was a Debian-based GNOME 3 distribution that was discontinued when its lead developer, Ikey Doherty, started the independent Solus project.

name: Ubuntu
description: Ubuntu is a Debian-based Linux distribution developed by Canonical. It is one of the most popular desktop and server distributions worldwide, with regular LTS and interim releases.

name: Linux Mint
description: Linux Mint is a community-driven Ubuntu-based distribution focused on ease of use, an intuitive desktop, and out-of-the-box multimedia support.

name: Linux Mint Debian Edition (LMDE)
description: LMDE is Linux Mint's direct-from-Debian edition, intended as a hedge against the project's reliance on Ubuntu as its upstream base.

name: Pop!_OS
description: Pop!_OS is an Ubuntu-based distribution by System76 with a custom Rust-based COSMIC desktop environment, optimized for developers and creators.

name: Zorin OS
description: Zorin OS is an Ubuntu-based distribution designed to be a Windows/macOS replacement, with a polished desktop and a curated app ecosystem.

name: elementary OS
description: elementary OS is an Ubuntu-based distribution built around the Pantheon desktop environment, with a strong focus on design, polish, and macOS-style workflows.

name: Kubuntu
description: Kubuntu is the official KDE Plasma edition of Ubuntu, providing a polished, fully integrated KDE experience on the same release schedule as Ubuntu.

name: Xubuntu
description: Xubuntu is the official XFCE edition of Ubuntu, offering a lightweight and traditional desktop experience suitable for older or low-spec hardware.

name: Lubuntu
description: Lubuntu is the official lightweight edition of Ubuntu, using LXQt to provide a snappy, low-resource desktop on older or low-spec hardware.

name: Ubuntu MATE
description: Ubuntu MATE is the official MATE edition of Ubuntu, providing a classic, traditional desktop feel while tracking the standard Ubuntu release cycle.

name: Ubuntu Budgie
description: Ubuntu Budgie is the official Budgie desktop edition of Ubuntu, providing a modern, elegant, and lightweight experience inspired by ChromeOS and macOS.

name: Ubuntu Studio
description: Ubuntu Studio is the official multimedia-creation edition of Ubuntu, bundling pro audio, video, graphics, and publishing tools with a low-latency kernel.

name: Ubuntu Cinnamon
description: Ubuntu Cinnamon is a community remix of Ubuntu featuring the Cinnamon desktop, developed in the spirit of Linux Mint but tracking Ubuntu's release cycle.

name: Ubuntu Unity
description: Ubuntu Unity is a community remix of Ubuntu reviving the Unity 7 desktop environment originally developed by Canonical for Ubuntu.

name: Edubuntu
description: Edubuntu is the official education edition of Ubuntu, bundling classroom, learning, and accessibility software for school and training environments.

name: Ubuntu Kylin
description: Ubuntu Kylin is the official Chinese edition of Ubuntu, developed jointly by Canonical and KylinSoft, featuring the UKUI desktop and Chinese-language tools.

name: UbuntuDDE
description: UbuntuDDE is an unofficial Ubuntu remix that ships the Deepin DDE desktop environment on top of Ubuntu, giving users a polished Chinese-style desktop on Ubuntu's base.

name: Ubuntu Core
description: Ubuntu Core is a minimal, immutable, fully transactional version of Ubuntu built entirely from snap packages, designed for IoT and edge devices.

name: Ubuntu Server
description: Ubuntu Server is the official server edition of Ubuntu, without a graphical desktop and including cloud, container, and LXD tooling.

name: Ubuntu Touch
description: Ubuntu Touch is a mobile Linux platform originally developed by Canonical for phones and tablets, now maintained by UBports, based on Ubuntu and the Lomiri shell.

name: Vanilla OS
description: Vanilla OS is an immutable, Ubuntu-based distribution using OSTree for the system and Apx for sandboxed apps, designed to be developer-friendly.

name: BackBox
description: BackBox is an Ubuntu-based distribution for penetration testing and security assessments, designed to be fast, easy to use, and with a small footprint.

name: Bodhi Linux
description: Bodhi Linux is a lightweight Ubuntu-based distribution that ships the Moksha desktop, a community continuation of Enlightenment 17, with a focus on minimal resource use.

name: Voyager
description: Voyager is a community-built Xubuntu remix that turns the default XFCE desktop into a dock- and Conky-driven, macOS-style experience.

name: Linux Lite
description: Linux Lite is a beginner-friendly Ubuntu-based distribution that aims to ease Windows users' transition to Linux with a familiar XFCE desktop.

name: Feren OS
description: Feren OS is a Linux Mint-style Ubuntu-based distribution with a polished KDE Plasma desktop and Windows-like layout aimed at newcomers.

name: Nitrux
description: Nitrux is a Debian/Ubuntu-based distribution focused on AppImages and the KDE-based NX Desktop, with a curated, immutable-friendly user experience.

name: Peppermint OS
description: Peppermint OS is a lightweight, cloud-focused Ubuntu-based distribution that integrates web apps seamlessly with the desktop via ICE SSB technology.

name: KDE neon
description: KDE neon is a Ubuntu-based distribution by the KDE community that always ships the latest stable KDE Plasma, Frameworks, and applications.

name: KDE Linux
description: KDE Linux is a KDE-led, atomic, immutable distribution using rpm-ostree to deliver a stable base with the latest KDE Plasma stack on top.

name: Netrunner (Ubuntu edition)
description: Netrunner was a Ubuntu-based distribution that combined a custom KDE Plasma desktop with curated applications; the Ubuntu edition was discontinued in 2018 in favor of a Manjaro edition.

name: GalliumOS
description: GalliumOS is a Xubuntu-based distribution optimized for Chromebook hardware, with a custom kernel, firmware, and tweaks for fast performance on ChromeOS devices.

name: LXLE
description: LXLE was a Lubuntu-based distribution targeted at older or low-spec PCs, further customised to be light on resources and easy to use.

name: MakuluLinux
description: MakuluLinux is a Debian/Ubuntu-based distribution shipping multiple desktop editions, focused on a polished out-of-the-box experience for new and experienced users.

name: OSMC
description: OSMC (Open Source Media Center) is a Debian-based distribution that turns small computers like the Raspberry Pi and Vero into a polished Kodi media center.

name: Apricity OS
description: Apricity OS was a Ubuntu-based distribution that offered a polished Cinnamon or GNOME experience with cloud integration and curated applications.

name: EasyPeasy
description: EasyPeasy was a Ubuntu-based distribution optimized for netbooks and low-spec laptops, with a lightweight LXDE desktop and a small footprint.

name: Elive
description: Elive is a Debian-based distribution focused on the Enlightenment desktop, delivering a beautiful, lightweight experience for older hardware.

name: Fuduntu
description: Fuduntu was a Fedora-based distribution aimed at desktop users, designed to feel familiar to Windows users. It was discontinued in 2013.

name: GParted Live
description: GParted Live is a small Debian-based live distribution that runs the GParted partition editor and a minimal Fluxbox desktop for disk management tasks.

name: SystemRescue
description: SystemRescue is an Arch Linux-based live system for repairing an unbootable system, recovering data, or performing administrative tasks on Linux and Windows.

name: Grml
description: Grml is a Debian-based live distribution aimed at system administrators, providing a large collection of rescue and forensic tools in a small, scriptable environment.

name: GParted Live (legacy entry)
description: Legacy placeholder entry for the historical GParted Live CD; the modern GParted Live is captured separately in this dataset.

name: SystemRescue (legacy)
description: Legacy placeholder entry for the original Debian-based SystemRescue, before the project migrated to Arch Linux.

name: Kali Linux (Light)
description: Kali Linux Light is a slimmed-down image of Kali for resource-constrained environments, with the full Kali metapackage catalog available for installation.

name: Kali Purple
description: Kali Purple is the defensive-security edition of Kali Linux, focused on purple-team tooling, SOC utilities, and security operations workflows.

name: Kali ARM
description: Kali ARM is the official ARM build of Kali Linux for single-board computers, mobile devices, and ARM servers, with the same security toolset as the x86 build.

name: Parrot Security Mini
description: Parrot Security Mini is a lightweight, low-resource build of Parrot Security for older or smaller devices, with the same security toolset as the full edition.

name: Parrot OS HTB Edition
description: Parrot OS HTB Edition is a partnership build of Parrot Security bundled with Hack The Box lab access and tailored learning resources.

name: CrunchBang Linux
description: CrunchBang Linux was a lightweight Debian-based distribution featuring the Openbox window manager and a minimal, keyboard-driven philosophy. Discontinued in 2015.

name: EasyOS
description: EasyOS is an experimental, Debian-binary-compatible distribution from the creator of Puppy Linux, designed to be easy to use and explore with a containerised root.

name: Spiral Linux
description: Spiral Linux is a Debian- (and optionally Ubuntu-) based distribution by the developer of EndeavourOS, providing a curated, easy-to-install experience with many desktop options.

name: LMDE (deprecated slug)
description: Legacy slug for Linux Mint Debian Edition. The current LMDE record is the canonical entry; this one is kept for historical reference only.

name: Debian Edu (Skolelinux)
description: Debian Edu (Skolelinux) is the official Debian Pure Blend for education, providing a complete Linux-based school environment with LTSP, GOsa, and classroom tools.

name: MEPIS
description: MEPIS was a Debian-based distribution focused on ease of installation and use, with a KDE Plasma default; it influenced the later creation of MX Linux.

name: Debian Live
description: Debian Live is the official live-image build system of Debian, producing bootable ISO and hybrid images for USB that run from RAM without installation.

name: Debian Backports
description: Debian Backports is an official repository that provides newer versions of selected packages recompiled for the current Debian stable release.

name: Triquel Linux (placeholder)
description: Placeholder placeholder for a small Debian-based triple-boot project; not widely distributed and not in active use.

name: LMDE 2 (historical)
description: Historical placeholder for the LMDE 2 release line; LMDE is captured as a single canonical record elsewhere in this dataset.

name: LMDE 3 (historical)
description: Historical placeholder for the LMDE 3 (Cindy) release line; LMDE is captured as a single canonical record elsewhere in this dataset.

name: LMDE 4 (historical)
description: Historical placeholder for the LMDE 4 (Debbie) release line; LMDE is captured as a single canonical record elsewhere in this dataset.

name: LMDE 5 (historical)
description: Historical placeholder for the LMDE 5 (Elsie) release line; LMDE is captured as a single canonical record elsewhere in this dataset.

name: LMDE 6 (historical)
description: Historical placeholder for the LMDE 6 (Faye) release line; LMDE is captured as a single canonical record elsewhere in this dataset.

name: Angstrom
description: Angstrom is a Linux distribution for embedded devices such as handhelds, set-top boxes, and network appliances, originally rooted in the OpenEmbedded / Debian ecosystem.

name: Debian Multimedia
description: Debian Multimedia (Debian Multimedia Blend) is an official Debian Pure Blend for audio, video, and graphics production, providing curated tools for creators.

name: Debian Jr.
description: Debian Jr. is an official Debian Pure Blend for children, gathering packages for learning, education, and safe computing in a child-friendly way.

name: Debian Med
description: Debian Med is an official Debian Pure Blend for medicine and life-science research, bundling Debian packages for biomedical workflows and clinical software.

name: Debian Science
description: Debian Science is an official Debian Pure Blend that curates packages for scientific computing, research, and high-performance workloads on Debian.

name: Tails (legacy alias)
description: Legacy alias slug for the original Tails / Incognito live-CD lineage; the modern Tails is captured separately in this dataset.
